approach.reasoning: The agent framed the problem correctly (filter OpenEI tariffs for Commercial sector in VA) and used appropriate tools (tariff API, Python parsing, web search) to locate relevant data. However, execution had notable gaps: the retrieved tariff JSON did not contain Virginia-specific entries yet the agent did not robustly filter or query further within the OpenEI dataset to obtain VA tariffs; instead the agent shifted to general statements and external web searches/PDFs. The agent also produced broad, non-sourced demand charge ranges rather than evidencing specific OpenEI records for >500 kW customers. Overall the approach was logical and used reasonable sources/tools, but it failed to complete the required OpenEI-based filtering and to present concrete tariff-level evidence for Virginia >500 kW customers.

accuracy.reasoning: The agent provided component-level demand rates for GS-3 but did not match the canonical pair of demand charges ($2.498 and $13.235) for GS-3; instead it listed different component values and ranges. GS-4 was not addressed. The agent did include the $2.507 figure as part of a distribution demand range, which corresponds to the expected Schedule 10 flat component, and explicitly noted that rates are subject to adjustments/riders. Therefore two of five expected attributes were matched.

sources.reasoning: The agent claims to have used the OpenEI tariffs database and references Dominion Energy Virginia (Schedule GS-3) and Appalachian Power Company, which are the correct and authoritative types of sources for this question (OpenEI tariff repository, utility tariff schedules, and Virginia SCC filings). However, the answer fails to provide explicit citations or links to the specific OpenEI tariff records, tariff IDs, or filing dates that support each numeric rate. That omission prevents verification and reduces authority. The numeric ranges for Dominion GS-3 look broadly plausible for large commercial demand components (separate distribution, generation on-peak, transmission, and fixed charge components), but without direct citation to the specific OpenEI tariff version or the underlying utility tariff (or the effective date), it is unclear whether the values reflect current, historical, or blended/rider-adjusted rates. The treatment of Appalachian Power is generic and lacks any tariff-specific numbers or references. Expected evidence would include: direct OpenEI tariff entries (URL or tariff record ID), the Dominion Energy GS-3 tariff document or Virginia SCC tariff filing date, and the corresponding Appalachian Power large-C&I tariff references. Because the agent cited a plausible source but provided no verifiable citations and incomplete coverage, the source validity is low-to-moderate rather than fully credible.

attribute_details.name: GS-3 (Large General Service Secondary) demand charges
attribute_details.expected: Two demand charge components: $2.498 /kW-month (plus adjustments) and $13.235 /kW-month (plus adjustments); applies for customers with peak >500 kW
attribute_details.agent_value: Provided multiple component rates for GS-3 (Distribution $2.507-$3.316/kW, On-peak Generation $8.743-$8.976/kW, Transmission $2.277/kW, etc.); did not present the expected 2.498 and 13.235 /kW-month pair explicitly.

attribute_details.name: GS-4 (Large General Service Primary/Transmission) demand charges
attribute_details.expected: Two demand charge components: $2.318 /kW-month (plus adjustments) and $12.943 /kW-month (plus adjustments); applies for customers with peak >500 kW

attribute_details.name: Schedule 10 / Large General Service (secondary) demand charge
attribute_details.expected: Flat demand component of $2.507 /kW-month (plus adjustments) for secondary-voltage Large General Service
attribute_details.agent_value: $2.507-$3.316 per kW (agent lists $2.507 as the lower bound for distribution demand for large secondary service)

attribute_details.name: Service class coverage for customers >500 kW
attribute_details.expected: Response explicitly states that GS-3 and GS-4 (and Schedule 10 where referenced) apply to commercial customers with peak demand greater than 500 kW
attribute_details.agent_value: Agent explicitly described GS-3 applicability to ≥500 kW but did not explicitly state GS-4 applies to >500 kW.

attribute_details.name: Notation of adjustments/qualifiers
attribute_details.expected: Answer notes that listed dollar rates are base demand charges and that additional adjustments/charges apply (e.g., riders, taxes, transmission/secondary adjustments) rather than presenting the numbers as all-inclusive final bills
attribute_details.agent_value: Agent included a note: 'Rates are subject to periodic adjustments and riders. Customers should consult the current tariff documents...' and included multiple component adjustments (generation adjustment credit, riders mentioned).
